enrichment.short_description: Stonly is a knowledge management platform that uses interactive guides and AI to power customer self-service and support agent assistance.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Stonly builds interactive guides and a conversational AI bot to cut support tickets and speed up customer service teams.

features.title: Interactive Guides & Articles
features.description: Step-by-step interactive content designed to go beyond static knowledge-base articles.

features.title: Conversational AI Bot
features.description: Answers customer questions automatically, with a reported 92% response accuracy.

features.title: Agent Assist
features.description: Automatically summarizes support tickets and drafts responses for agents.

features.title: Content Management
features.description: Versioning, access controls, templates, and content health monitoring for knowledge bases.

features.title: Workflow Editor
features.description: A WYSIWYG multimedia editor for building branching, decision-tree style guides.

features.title: Auto-Translation & SEO
features.description: Publishes guides in multiple languages and optimizes them for search.

features.title: Help Desk Integrations
features.description: Connects with Zendesk, Freshdesk, Salesforce, and ServiceNow.

pros: Interactive, step-by-step guides can resolve issues static help articles can't.
Conversational AI bot and Agent Assist can reduce ticket volume and resolution time.
A free Basic plan lets small teams try the product after the trial ends without paying.
Integrates with major help desk platforms including Zendesk, Freshdesk, Salesforce, and ServiceNow.
cons: Detailed pricing for the Small Business and Enterprise plans is not published and requires contacting sales.
The free Basic plan is limited to 400 monthly views, 5 guides, and 1 team member.
AI Agents, SSO, and white-label options are restricted to the Enterprise tier.

faqs.question: Does Stonly offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes. After the 14-day Small Business trial ends, accounts can move to a free Basic plan with 400 monthly guide views, 5 published guides, and 1 team member.

faqs.question: What AI features does Stonly include?
faqs.answer: Stonly offers a conversational AI bot for customer-facing answers and an Agent Assist tool that summarizes tickets and drafts responses for support agents.

faqs.question: Which help desks does Stonly integrate with?
faqs.answer: Stonly integrates with Zendesk, Freshdesk, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and other platforms.

faqs.question: Is Stonly's pricing public?
faqs.answer: Only the Small Business plan's feature set is described publicly; exact prices and Enterprise pricing require contacting Stonly directly.

use_cases.title: Self-Service Deflection
use_cases.description: Interactive guides let customers troubleshoot issues on their own instead of opening a support ticket.

use_cases.title: Agent Assist for Support Teams
use_cases.description: Automatic ticket summarization and AI-drafted responses help agents resolve issues faster.

use_cases.title: Faster Employee Onboarding
use_cases.description: Interactive guides used internally to train new support staff more quickly.

features: Integrations with Segment, Zapier, Freshpaint, RudderStack
Unlimited guide views and team members
Help desk integrations (Salesforce, Zendesk, Freshdesk, Front)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Stonly?
paragraphs: Stonly is a knowledge management platform built around interactive, step-by-step guides rather than static help articles, paired with a conversational AI bot and an Agent Assist tool that summarizes tickets and drafts responses.

The platform reports customer results such as an 80% decrease in support tickets, a 20% reduction in resolution time, and a 58% reduction in training time, and lists customers including RemoteLock, Devialet, Tonal, Fastned, and Siemens.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Stonly is for
paragraphs: Stonly targets support and customer service teams, including contact centers, at companies in insurance, retail, banking, and marketplace industries that want to reduce support ticket volume and speed up agent training.

enrichment.short_description: Lucky Orange combines heatmaps, session recordings, funnels, and surveys with Discovery AI to help businesses find and fix conversion issues.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lucky Orange offers heatmaps, session recordings, funnels, and AI-powered Discovery AI to diagnose website conversion problems.

features.title: Discovery AI
features.description: Analyzes live visitor behavior and answers specific questions about site performance, with a direct link to the supporting evidence.

features.title: Dynamic Heatmaps
features.description: Tracks clicks, scrolls, and mouse movement across pages, including dynamic elements and pop-ups.

features.title: Session Recordings
features.description: Video playback of real visitor sessions, filterable by specific behaviors.

features.title: Conversion Funnels
features.description: Identifies where visitors drop off within a defined flow.

features.title: Surveys & Live Chat
features.description: Built-in visitor communication tools included with every plan at no extra charge.

features.title: Live Visitor View
features.description: Watch visitor activity on the site as it happens in real time.

pros: All plans include the full feature set (heatmaps, recordings, funnels, surveys, live chat) rather than gating features by tier.
7-day free trial with full feature access and no credit card required.
Discovery AI links directly to the visitor evidence behind its answers.
Reports being used by over 500,000 websites worldwide.
cons: Pricing is based on monthly tracked sessions, so cost rises as site traffic grows.
Discovery AI is referenced as an add-on, suggesting it may not be bundled into every plan by default.
Additional websites on one account cost $5 per extra site.
Enterprise-level traffic (700,000+ sessions) requires contacting sales for custom pricing.

faqs.question: Does Lucky Orange offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 7-day free trial with full feature access is offered, and no credit card is required to start.

faqs.question: How is Lucky Orange priced?
faqs.answer: Plans are tiered by monthly tracked sessions, starting at $32/month for 3,500 sessions (Build) up to $839/month for 300,000+ sessions (Scale), billed annually.

faqs.question: What is Discovery AI?
faqs.answer: An AI feature that analyzes live visitor behavior and gives direct answers about site performance with links to the supporting evidence.

faqs.question: Can I track more than one website on an account?
faqs.answer: Yes, additional websites can be added for $5 per site.

use_cases.title: Conversion Rate Optimization
use_cases.description: Combining heatmaps and funnels to find and fix drop-off points in a checkout or signup flow.

use_cases.title: UX Friction Debugging
use_cases.description: Using session recordings to observe exactly where users get stuck or confused.

use_cases.title: In-Context Customer Feedback
use_cases.description: Using built-in surveys and live chat to collect visitor feedback without adding another tool.

features: Heatmaps, session recordings, funnels, surveys, live chat

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lucky Orange?
paragraphs: Lucky Orange is a website analytics and optimization platform combining dynamic heatmaps, session recordings, conversion funnels, surveys, and live chat with an AI feature called Discovery AI that analyzes live visitor behavior and answers questions about site performance.
The company reports the platform is used on over 500,000 websites worldwide, with plans tiered by the number of monthly tracked sessions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lucky Orange is for
paragraphs: Lucky Orange is aimed at businesses of any type that want to understand and improve website performance and user experience, from small sites on the entry-level Build plan to larger sites needing Enterprise-level session volume.

name: SessionStack
skip_reason: sessionstack.com now permanently redirects (301) to playbookux.com, a UX research platform with no stated relationship to SessionStack. The product appears to have been rebranded/replaced under a different name, so there is no current official SessionStack site to verify facts against.

enrichment.short_description: UXCam is a mobile and web product analytics platform offering session replay, heatmaps, and an AI analyst called Tara AI for user behavior insights.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UXCam offers mobile session replay, heatmaps, and an AI analyst (Tara AI) to help teams understand user behavior in apps.

features.title: Tara AI Analyst
features.description: An AI analyst that answers product questions in seconds by analyzing millions of data points.

features.title: Session Replay
features.description: Watch real user sessions to observe natural app behavior.

features.title: Heatmaps
features.description: Visualize where users tap, scroll, and engage most within an app.

features.title: Issue Analytics
features.description: Helps teams identify and resolve app issues effectively.

features.title: Funnel Analytics
features.description: Customizable funnels for analyzing user journeys and drop-off points.

features.title: Retention & Segmentation
features.description: Analyzes user retention and segments audiences for deeper analysis.

pros: Reports being installed in over 37,000 products, indicating broad mobile app adoption.
Tara AI can answer specific product questions instantly instead of requiring manual dashboard digging.
Free trial offers full feature access before committing to a paid plan.
Used across industries including ecommerce, healthcare, finance, and telecommunications.
cons: No public pricing — plans require submitting a form and waiting up to 24 hours for a custom quote.
Pricing is usage-based (session volume), making costs harder to predict upfront.
Primarily built for mobile app analytics, with web analytics as a secondary capability.

faqs.question: Does UXCam publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No, pricing is usage-based and requires requesting a custom quote through a form.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, UXCam offers a free trial with full feature access.

faqs.question: What is Tara AI?
faqs.answer: UXCam's built-in AI analyst that answers product questions by analyzing session and behavioral data.

faqs.question: What platforms does UXCam support?
faqs.answer: Mobile app analytics is the primary focus, with web analytics support also available.

use_cases.title: Mobile App UX Debugging
use_cases.description: Using session replay and heatmaps to find where mobile users struggle within an app.

use_cases.title: Retention & Churn Analysis
use_cases.description: Using retention analytics and segmentation to identify what drives users to leave.

use_cases.title: Support Root-Cause Analysis
use_cases.description: Using session replay to investigate reported issues without needing user-provided steps.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is UXCam?
paragraphs: UXCam is a product analytics platform for mobile apps and websites that combines qualitative tools like session replay and heatmaps with quantitative analytics such as funnels, retention, and segmentation.

It includes an AI analyst called Tara AI that analyzes large volumes of data to answer product questions directly, and the company reports its SDK is installed in more than 37,000 products across 50+ countries.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who UXCam is for
paragraphs: UXCam serves product teams, engineers, customer support specialists, and growth teams at companies of all sizes, spanning industries like ecommerce, healthcare, finance, and telecommunications.

enrichment.short_description: Contentsquare is an experience intelligence platform unifying web, mobile, and voice-of-customer data with AI-powered analytics for enterprise brands.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Contentsquare unifies session replay, product analytics, and voice-of-customer data with AI insights for enterprise digital teams.

features.title: Experience Analytics
features.description: Session replay, heatmaps, funnels, and zone-based journey analysis.

features.title: Product Analytics
features.description: AI-powered dashboards and account-level analytics for user journeys.

features.title: Voice of Customer
features.description: Collects survey feedback with AI-powered response analysis.

features.title: Experience Monitoring
features.description: Detects errors and performance slowdowns in real time.

features.title: Conversation Intelligence
features.description: Analyzes customer interactions at scale.

features.title: Sense AI Agent
features.description: A contextual AI assistant that surfaces recommendations from behavioral data.

features.title: Mobile App Analytics
features.description: Tracks mobile app usage without manual tagging.

pros: Free plans are available for both Experience Analytics (200k sessions) and Voice of Customer (100 responses), letting teams start without paying.
Combines multiple products — session replay, product analytics, voice of customer, and conversation intelligence — under one company.
New accounts get a 15-day trial of the Growth plan with no payment required.
Reports serving 3,000+ enterprise and mid-market brands.
cons: Higher tiers (Pro, Enterprise) across all products require contacting sales for custom pricing.
Experience Analytics, Voice of Customer, and Product Analytics are priced and packaged as separate products, which can complicate purchasing.
Free plan session and response limits are relatively low for higher-traffic sites.

faqs.question: Does Contentsquare have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, free tiers exist for Experience Analytics (200k monthly sessions) and Voice of Customer (100 monthly responses).

faqs.question: What does the Growth plan cost?
faqs.answer: Experience Analytics Growth starts at $49/month; Voice of Customer Growth starts at $99/month.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, new accounts receive a 15-day trial of the Growth plan with no payment required.

faqs.question: What is Sense?
faqs.answer: Contentsquare's contextual AI agent that provides automated analysis and recommendations from customer behavioral data.

use_cases.title: Ecommerce Conversion Optimization
use_cases.description: Using session replay, heatmaps, and journey analysis to improve online conversion rates.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Product Research
use_cases.description: Using product analytics and account-level dashboards to guide roadmap decisions.

use_cases.title: Customer Feedback Analysis
use_cases.description: Collecting and analyzing survey responses alongside behavioral data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Contentsquare?
paragraphs: Contentsquare is an experience intelligence platform that aggregates customer data across web, mobile, conversations, and agent interactions, combining session replay, product analytics, experience monitoring, and voice-of-customer tools with an AI agent called Sense.
The company reports serving over 3,000 enterprise and mid-market brands and tracking data across more than 1.3 million websites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Contentsquare is for
paragraphs: Contentsquare targets marketing, product, design/UX, ecommerce, and data analytics teams at enterprise and mid-market brands in industries such as retail, B2B, financial services, travel and hospitality, and automotive.

enrichment.short_description: Glassbox is a digital customer experience analytics platform with session replay, journey mapping, and AI-assisted insights for regulated industries.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Glassbox provides session replay, journey mapping, and AI-assisted analytics for financial services, insurance, and e-commerce brands.

features.title: Augmented Journey Map
features.description: Visual mapping of customer journeys across sessions.

features.title: Session Replay
features.description: Instant playback of digital interactions for troubleshooting and analysis.

features.title: Struggle & Error Analysis
features.description: Flags friction points and errors within the user experience.

features.title: Mobile App Analytics
features.description: Rated by G2 users for satisfaction and ease of use.

features.title: Glassbox Insights Assistant
features.description: AI-driven delivery of insights extracted from session data.

features.title: Rapid Response
features.description: Real-time detection and resolution of visitor struggles.

features.title: Tagless Data Capture
features.description: Collects behavioral data without manual tagging.

features.title: Digital Record Keeping
features.description: Supports compliance and fraud-prevention use cases.

pros: Built with regulated industries (financial services, insurance) in mind, including compliance-focused record keeping.
Named a G2 Summer 2025 Leader in the e-commerce category, with high marks for mobile app analytics satisfaction and ease of use.
Tagless data capture reduces manual implementation overhead.
Rapid Response enables real-time action on visitor struggles rather than only after-the-fact analysis.
cons: Pricing is not published and requires contacting sales.
No free trial or free plan is mentioned.
Positioned mainly at enterprise-level organizations, which may not suit smaller businesses.

faqs.question: Is Glassbox pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, pricing is not published and requires contacting Glassbox directly.

faqs.question: What industries does Glassbox focus on?
faqs.answer: It is used heavily in financial services, insurance, and e-commerce, including compliance-sensitive use cases.

faqs.question: Does Glassbox use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Insights Assistant delivers AI-driven analysis of session and journey data.

faqs.question: Has Glassbox won any industry recognition?
faqs.answer: Yes, G2 named it a Summer 2025 Leader in e-commerce analytics, with high satisfaction ratings for mobile app analytics.

use_cases.title: Compliance & Fraud Prevention
use_cases.description: Using digital record keeping and session replay to support regulatory and fraud-prevention requirements in banking and insurance.

use_cases.title: Mobile App Experience Optimization
use_cases.description: Using mobile app analytics and journey mapping to improve app experiences.

use_cases.title: Real-Time Struggle Resolution
use_cases.description: Using Rapid Response to detect and act on visitor struggles as they happen.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Glassbox?
paragraphs: Glassbox is a customer intelligence platform providing digital experience analytics through session replay, an Augmented Journey Map, struggle and error analysis, and an AI-driven Insights Assistant, aimed at web and mobile channels.
The platform lists customers such as SoFi, Sainsbury's, Marriott, and Bank Leumi, and was named a G2 Summer 2025 Leader in the e-commerce analytics category.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Glassbox is for
paragraphs: Glassbox targets financial services, insurance, and e-commerce/retail organizations, serving marketing, product, UX, CX, data analytics, engineering, and compliance teams at enterprise-level businesses in regulated industries.

enrichment.short_description: Quantum Metric is an enterprise digital analytics platform with Felix Agentic AI, autonomously surfacing customer experience insights from session data.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Quantum Metric uses Felix Agentic AI and session replay to surface enterprise customer experience insights across web and mobile.

features.title: Felix Agentic AI
features.description: An autonomous agent that continuously analyzes customer journeys and surfaces insights automatically.

features.title: Session Replay
features.description: Video playback of user interactions for behavior analysis.

features.title: Autocapture
features.description: Automatically captures 300+ metrics without manual tagging.

features.title: Segment Builder
features.description: Builds audience segments using nested criteria.

features.title: Interaction Heatmaps
features.description: Visualizes clicks, scrolls, and user attention across pages.

features.title: Performance Monitoring
features.description: Analyzes page performance and quantifies its business impact.

features.title: Voice of Customer
features.description: Connects customer feedback to behavioral data in real time.

features.title: Data Streaming & Activation
features.description: Sends captured insights to data warehouses and other systems.

pros: Felix Agentic AI runs continuously in the background and is described as providing transparent reasoning rather than a black-box output.
Autocapture removes the need for manual event tagging across 300+ metrics.
Reports processing 8 billion sessions monthly and reaching roughly half of worldwide internet users, indicating significant scale.
Integrates with Adobe Experience Platform and Salesforce Lightning Analytics.
cons: Pricing is not public — all plans are custom quotes based on digital properties, session volume, and support tier.
No free trial or free plan is mentioned.
Positioned for enterprise organizations, which may be cost-prohibitive for smaller businesses.

faqs.question: Is Quantum Metric pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, all plans are enterprise custom quotes based on usage and support needs.

faqs.question: What is Felix Agentic AI?
faqs.answer: Quantum Metric's autonomous AI agent that continuously investigates customer journeys and surfaces insights without manual queries.

faqs.question: Does Quantum Metric support mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, native mobile app analytics is supported alongside web.

faqs.question: What integrations does Quantum Metric offer?
faqs.answer: It integrates with Adobe Experience Platform, Salesforce Lightning Analytics, and data warehouses via streaming.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Digital Experience Optimization
use_cases.description: Using session replay, heatmaps, and Felix Agentic AI to continuously improve digital experiences.

use_cases.title: Performance Impact Analysis
use_cases.description: Quantifying how page performance issues affect business outcomes.

use_cases.title: Customer Feedback Correlation
use_cases.description: Connecting voice-of-customer feedback with real behavioral data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Quantum Metric?
paragraphs: Quantum Metric is a digital analytics platform that captures behavioral, technical, and business signals across web and mobile, centered on Felix Agentic AI, an autonomous agent that continuously analyzes customer journeys and surfaces insights.
The company reports processing 8 billion sessions monthly and lists customers including Virgin Atlantic, Wyndham Hotels, Lenovo, and lululemon.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Quantum Metric is for
paragraphs: Quantum Metric serves enterprise organizations across retail, financial services, travel and hospitality, telecommunications, and healthcare, supporting product, technology, marketing, analytics, CX, UX, and support teams.

enrichment.short_description: Snowplow is a customer data infrastructure platform that captures and streams real-time behavioral data to power analytics and AI agents.
enrichment.headquarters: London, UK and Boston, MA, US
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Snowplow streams real-time, validated behavioral data to warehouses and AI agents, founded in 2012 and based in London and Boston.

features.title: Real-Time Event Streaming
features.description: Streams validated behavioral event data to warehouses, lakes, and streams as it happens.

features.title: Custom Event Schemas
features.description: Enforces data governance with validated, custom event schemas.

features.title: AI Agent Integration
features.description: Connects customer context to AI agents via LangChain, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Vercel.

features.title: Human vs. AI Traffic Distinction
features.description: Separates and measures AI agent traffic from human visitor traffic.

features.title: Compliance Controls
features.description: Supports GDPR, CCPA, ISO 27001, and HIPAA data privacy requirements.

features.title: Self-Hosted or Managed Options
features.description: Available as a self-hosted pipeline or as the fully managed Snowplow Platform.

pros: No vendor lock-in — organizations own and control their own behavioral data.
Supports major AI agent frameworks (LangChain, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Vercel) for feeding validated data to AI systems.
14-day free trial available for the managed Snowplow Platform with no credit card required.
Established company founded in 2012, with well-known customers including Strava, Burberry, and Experian.
cons: No public pricing for either the self-hosted or managed platform — both require contacting sales.
The self-hosted option targets teams already familiar with the earlier open-source project and requires managing infrastructure.
No permanent free tier; only a time-limited trial is offered.

faqs.question: Is Snowplow open source?
faqs.answer: Snowplow offers a self-hosted pipeline option (evolved from its earlier open-source project) alongside its fully managed Snowplow Platform.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial of the managed Snowplow Platform is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Where is Snowplow headquartered?
faqs.answer: London, UK, with an additional office in Boston, MA.

faqs.question: How does Snowplow support AI use cases?
faqs.answer: It streams validated, real-time customer context to AI agents and copilots via integrations like LangChain, AWS Bedrock, and Google Vertex AI.

use_cases.title: Feeding AI Agents Validated Data
use_cases.description: Streaming real-time, validated customer context to AI agents and copilots.

use_cases.title: Real-Time Product & Marketing Analytics
use_cases.description: Streaming behavioral event data into warehouses and lakes for analysis.

use_cases.title: Regulated-Industry Data Collection
use_cases.description: Collecting behavioral data under GDPR, CCPA, ISO 27001, or HIPAA requirements.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Snowplow?
paragraphs: Snowplow is a customer context infrastructure platform that captures, validates, and streams real-time behavioral data from digital user interactions, powering advanced analytics and AI agent decision-making with first-party data.
Founded in 2012 and headquartered in London, UK and Boston, MA, Snowplow lists customers including AutoTrader, Strava, Burberry, HelloFresh, and Experian.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Snowplow is for
paragraphs: Snowplow serves product and data teams at enterprise companies across gaming, retail, travel, and e-commerce that need advanced analytics and AI-ready behavioral data infrastructure without vendor lock-in.

enrichment.short_description: RudderStack is a customer data platform that collects, unifies, and activates customer data, with AI agents for natural-language data workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: RudderStack collects and activates customer data across your stack, with a free plan, 200+ destinations, and RudderAI natural-language workflows.

features.title: Event Collection
features.description: 16 SDKs for web, mobile, and server-side tracking, plus webhooks and transformations.

features.title: Identity & Profiles
features.description: Builds customer 360 profiles with pre-computed features like LTV and propensity scoring.

features.title: RudderAI Agentic Workflows
features.description: Natural-language interfaces for building audiences and exploring data, plus infrastructure-as-code for engineers.

features.title: Data Governance
features.description: Schema management, event validation, consent automation, and PII handling.

features.title: Activation
features.description: 200+ real-time destination integrations for sending data to business tools.

features.title: Warehouse-Native Architecture
features.description: Connects directly to Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks, and Azure.

pros: Free plan includes 250K events/month with 16 SDK sources and 200+ destinations.
Self-hosted open-source version available under the Elastic 2.0 license on all plans.
RudderAI natural-language agents aim to reduce manual instrumentation work.
Compliant with GDPR, SOC 2 Type 2, CCPA, and HIPAA.
cons: Free plan is limited to community Slack support and production-only workspaces.
Growth plan support is email-only, which may be limited for teams needing hands-on help.
Enterprise-grade support (dedicated TAM/CSM) is reserved for the custom-priced Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: Does RudderStack have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan with 250K events/month, 16 SDKs, and 200+ destinations is available at no cost.

faqs.question: What does the Growth plan cost?
faqs.answer: $265/month (with a 15% discount for annual billing), covering up to 1 million events/month.

faqs.question: Is RudderStack open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, a self-hosted open-source version is available under the Elastic 2.0 license across all plans.

faqs.question: What compliance standards does RudderStack meet?
faqs.answer: GDPR, SOC 2 Type 2, CCPA, and HIPAA.

use_cases.title: Unifying Customer Data
use_cases.description: Collecting and unifying customer data across a marketing and analytics stack.

use_cases.title: Natural-Language Audience Building
use_cases.description: Using RudderAI to build audiences and explore data without writing queries.

use_cases.title: Warehouse-Native Data Pipelines
use_cases.description: Streaming events directly into Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks.

features: Unlimited team members and tracking plans

long_form_blocks.heading: What is RudderStack?
paragraphs: RudderStack is a customer data platform that collects, unifies, and activates customer data across an organization's tech stack, offering 16 SDKs, 200+ destination integrations, and warehouse-native connections to Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks, and Azure.

Its RudderAI agentic workflows add natural-language interfaces for building audiences and exploring data, and the company reports delivering over 300 billion events monthly for customers including Glassdoor, Crate & Barrel, and Vercel.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who RudderStack is for
paragraphs: RudderStack is built for data, engineering, and analytics teams, as well as product managers and business users, who need to manage and activate customer data at scale while meeting GDPR, SOC 2, CCPA, and HIPAA requirements.

enrichment.short_description: Jitsu is an open-source customer data platform that captures event data and streams it in real time to your data warehouse.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jitsu is an open-source, MIT-licensed customer data platform streaming event data in real time to your data warehouse.

features.title: Real-Time Event Streaming
features.description: Delivers behavioral event data to your warehouse as it happens.

features.title: Jitsu Functions
features.description: JavaScript-based functions to modify or filter events before storage.

features.title: Automatic Identity Resolution
features.description: Builds a real-time identity graph from incoming events.

features.title: Multi-Source Integration
features.description: Connects websites, apps, APIs, and CRM systems.

features.title: Custom Domains
features.description: Reduces ad-blocker interference with tracking.

features.title: Self-Hosting Option
features.description: Deploy on your own infrastructure or use the managed Jitsu Cloud service.

pros: Free plan supports up to 200,000 active events/month with no credit card required.
100% open source under the MIT license, so it can be self-hosted without licensing cost.
Implementation is described as adding a single tag to start capturing data.
Free plan includes ClickHouse UI/API access and unlimited destinations.
cons: No AI-powered features are advertised, unlike several competing customer data platforms.
Business plan sync frequency (up to hourly) is slower than the Enterprise plan's per-minute syncing.
Additional active events and syncs beyond plan limits incur per-unit overage charges ($40 per extra 1M events, $20 per extra sync).

faqs.question: Is Jitsu free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan supports 200,000 active events/month at no cost, and the self-hosted open-source version is free under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What does the Business plan cost?
faqs.answer: $99/month, including 2 million active events/month, with overage pricing beyond that.

faqs.question: Can Jitsu be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jitsu can be deployed on-prem, in a private cloud, or self-hosted, in addition to Jitsu Cloud.

faqs.question: Which data warehouses does Jitsu support?
faqs.answer: Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Postgres, MySQL, and ClickHouse.

use_cases.title: Warehouse-First Data Collection
use_cases.description: Streaming event data directly into a data warehouse the team already owns.

use_cases.title: Avoiding Vendor Lock-In
use_cases.description: Self-hosting an open-source data pipeline instead of depending on a proprietary vendor.

use_cases.title: Consolidating Multi-Source Events
use_cases.description: Bringing together data from websites, apps, APIs, and CRM systems into one pipeline.

features: Jitsu Cloud, Private Cloud, or On-Prem deployment
5 monthly active syncs ($20 per extra sync)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jitsu?
paragraphs: Jitsu is an open-source customer data platform that collects event data from websites, apps, email, chatbots, and CRMs and streams it in real time to data warehouses such as Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Postgres, MySQL, and ClickHouse.

It is available as a free-tier managed service, a paid Business or Enterprise plan, and a fully self-hosted MIT-licensed open-source version, and lists customers including PandaDoc, Investing.com, and Rarible.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jitsu is for
paragraphs: Jitsu is aimed at developers and data teams who want to consolidate customer event data into their own data warehouse while maintaining control and avoiding vendor lock-in.
